description_long: This is a striking orange flower with recurved petals, each petal adorned with dark spots, resembling the Tiger Lily (Lilium lancifolium). The flower has six prominent petals that curve backward, exposing long, slender stamens with dark anthers extending outward from the center. The central style is also visible, protruding slightly beyond the stamens. The stem is green and upright, supporting the flower at its apex, and is accompanied by a few lanceolate green leaves spaced along its length. The overall structure is delicate, with the flower head appearing large relative to the slender stem. The coloration and petal arrangement make it a visually distinctive and ornamental plant, often found in urban parks or gardens.
description: A vibrant orange flower with recurved, spotted petals and long, protruding stamens atop a slender green stem with lanceolate leaves. The flower is visually striking and ornamental.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face of the flower with all six recurved, spotted petals and the central arrangement of stamens and style.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the backward curve of the petals and the prominent extension of the stamens and style.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the recurved petals and the attachment point to the stem, with stamens visible from behind.
description_view_3: Angled side-rear view emphasizing the curvature of the petals and the spatial arrangement of the stamens.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial, whorled configuration with six distinct petals curving backward symmetrically around the central axis. There is clear radial symmetry and no significant overlapping or layering.
